qualityGate.scope: TokenStack Command Center final Ultragoal gate

aiSlopCleaner.evidence: Final marker scan over .omx/ultragoal/evidence, src, src-tauri/src, scripts, tests, docs, README.md, and .github/workflows/ci.yml found no unwrap_or_default, TODO, FIXME, hack, bypass, or silent default hits.

verification.evidence: Fresh verification passed: 5 frontend tests, 1 browser screenshot test with no console errors, 39 Rust no-default tests, 41 Rust app/core tests with the local Tauri GTK/WebKit sysroot, clippy with -D warnings in both Rust modes, secret and fixture scans, production build, and Tauri packaging smoke.

codeReview.evidence: Independent code-reviewer and architect subagents rechecked the final staged candidate after timeout, provenance, failed-run coverage, refresh serialization, SQL removal, CSP, evidence count, and packaging fixes.

codeReviewer.evidence: No blocking issues found; verified bounded connector timeouts, endpoint provenance, failed-run coverage downgrade, backend refresh lock, SQL removal, CSP, final evidence counts, and staged diff checks.

architect.evidence: Timeout blocker fixed; architecture blockers for connector provenance, failed-refresh coverage downgrade, and refresh serialization remain fixed.

architectureInvariantGate.evidence: Every non-negotiable safety invariant from the PRD brief has implementation, test, and independent review proof in the final staged candidate.

invariants.invariant: Never call any endpoint whose path contains `/consume`.
invariants.implementationEvidence: SafetyGuard rejects normalized paths containing /consume before request construction; connector endpoints are registered read-only specs and no authenticated connector bypasses the guard.
invariants.testEvidence: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --no-default-features passed rejects_any_path_containing_consume and connector guard tests; full Tauri-mode cargo test also passed.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Code-reviewer APPROVE and architect CLEAR both confirmed the SafetyGuard path and no SQL/frontend bypass remains.

invariants.invariant: Never consume, redeem, claim, mutate, or spend reset credits.
invariants.implementationEvidence: Authenticated connector specs allow GET only, reject request bodies, and expose reset-credit data through read-only connectors for /wham/rate-limit-reset-credits and /backend-api/rate_limits.
invariants.testEvidence: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --no-default-features passed non-read-only method and request-body rejection tests; final 39 no-default and 41 Tauri-mode Rust tests passed.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Independent review verified no mutation endpoint support, no /consume path, and Rust-owned connector execution.

invariants.invariant: Never print, store, log, display, fixture, or commit auth tokens, account secrets, or full auth file contents.
invariants.implementationEvidence: Auth data stays in AuthHandle/SecretString, public errors are redacted, connector results persist redacted status/error metadata, and fixture policy uses synthetic data only.
invariants.testEvidence: pnpm secret:scan and pnpm fixture:scan passed; Rust redaction, auth serialization, and persistence tests passed in the no-default and Tauri-mode suites.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Code-reviewer APPROVE confirmed auth material is not serialized to frontend DTOs, SQLite rows, logs, fixtures, or screenshots.

invariants.invariant: All auth-adjacent local reads are read-only, path-guarded, redacted, and performed in the Tauri/Rust safety boundary.
invariants.implementationEvidence: Auth locator and parser are Rust-side, allowlisted, redacted, and never expose raw auth contents to frontend IPC or local persistence.
invariants.testEvidence: Rust auth-adjacent local read tests passed with cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --no-default-features and with the Tauri sysroot default-feature test run.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Architect CLEAR confirmed Rust remains the safety/data boundary and the frontend SQL plugin was removed.

invariants.invariant: Undocumented read-only endpoints are allowed and enabled by default, but only through audited connector code, endpoint registry entries, response schemas, and safety checks.
invariants.implementationEvidence: UndocumentedRateLimitsConnector is enabled through the audited endpoint registry for /backend-api/rate_limits, uses bounded_http_client, validates response shape, and persists endpoint provenance.
invariants.testEvidence: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --no-default-features passed connector schema/provenance tests including refresh_persists_imported_history_for_later_summary_calls and known_reset_credit_timeout_returns_failed_result.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Code-reviewer APPROVE verified undocumented read-only provenance and bounded timeout behavior; architect CLEAR verified the connector architecture constraints.

invariants.invariant: Auth tokens never cross the Rust-to-frontend IPC boundary.
invariants.implementationEvidence: Frontend calls typed Tauri commands for summaries and refreshes; command DTOs expose availability, coverage, connector, and aggregate data without token fields.
invariants.testEvidence: pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:browser, Rust auth serialization tests, and secret scan passed after removing tauri-plugin-sql and narrowing Tauri capabilities to core:default.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Independent reviewers confirmed frontend SQL removal, restrictive CSP, and no raw-auth IPC surface.

invariants.invariant: Local SQLite never stores raw auth secrets or full auth files.
invariants.implementationEvidence: SQLite migrations store usage events, source coverage, connector runs, reset-credit batches, and rate-limit windows; connector failures and auth metadata are redacted before persistence.
invariants.testEvidence: Rust persistence tests passed, including connector_runs_roundtrip_redacted_errors, reset_credit_batches_roundtrip, rate-limit window persistence, and auth_file_contents_not_persisted coverage; pnpm secret:scan passed.
invariants.reviewEvidence: Code-reviewer APPROVE and architect CLEAR confirmed persisted connector result provenance and redacted storage behavior.

verificationDetails.result: passed: 1 Chromium screenshot test, no console errors; refreshed dark/light screenshots
passed: no auth-like token patterns found
passed: fixtures are synthetic and redacted
passed: 41 Rust app/core tests plus Tauri binary target with 0 tests

safetyInvariants: Authenticated connector requests are Rust-owned and pass through SafetyGuard before network execution.

SafetyGuard rejects /consume paths, non-read-only methods, request bodies, unregistered endpoints, unsafe hosts, plaintext production transport, missing schemas, and missing read-only review metadata.
Auth material remains in AuthHandle/SecretString and is not serialized to frontend DTOs, SQLite rows, logs, fixtures, or screenshots.
Frontend SQL plugin/capability is removed; Tauri capability is core:default and CSP is restrictive rather than null.
Connector HTTP clients have bounded connect/request timeouts so a stalled remote endpoint cannot hold the backend refresh lock indefinitely.
Backend refreshes are serialized by a process-local mutex; UI refreshes are de-duplicated and auto-refresh backs off after failures.
Latest failed connector runs downgrade coverage/confidence while preserving last-good reset-credit and rate-limit rows without double-counting repeated successes.

codeReviewer.summary: No blocking issues found; verified bounded timeouts, provenance, degraded coverage after failed refresh, backend refresh lock, SQL removal, CSP, evidence counts, and staged diff checks.

architect.summary: Timeout blocker fixed; prior architecture blockers for provenance, failed-refresh coverage downgrade, and refresh serialization remain fixed.

packaging.localSmoke: pnpm tauri:build passed with extracted local Linux sysroot; no host packages were installed.
packaging.windowsInstaller: Configured in .github/workflows/ci.yml as windows-latest x86_64-pc-windows-msvc Tauri build smoke; local Linux host cannot emit the Windows NSIS artifact.

screenshots.visualInspection: Both screenshots are nonblank and show the dense Command Center dashboard in dark/light themes.

cleanupScan.notes: Final marker scan over source, docs, workflow, tests, and evidence found no unwrap_or_default, TODO, FIXME, hack, bypass, or silent default hits.

residualRisk: Windows NSIS installer artifact creation is delegated to the configured Windows CI runner rather than produced on this Linux host.
Unsigned public release signing remains approval-gated and outside this local smoke gate.
